html:
Exclusive to:

The Soothing Tea is a recipe exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in the Return of Them update. It is made with 1 Forget-Me-Lots, 1 Honey or Honeycomb, 1 Ice, and 1 Filler that cannot be Meats, Fish, Monster Food, Egg, Dairy, Inedible or Vegetable. A convenient filler would be more of the required ingredients or Fruit. The Forget-Me-Lots can be fresh or Dried Forget-Me-Lots.

It restores 3 health and 15 sanity and an additional 30 sanity over 1 minute. It increases the temperature perceived by the player by 1 degree per second over 5 seconds.

Recipe

Filler cannot be Meats, Fish, Monster Food, Egg, Dairy, Inedible or Vegetable.

Examples

Tips

  • Except for Ice Cream, this dish has the highest efficiency in terms of sanity gain per honey.
  • Soothing Tea is a useful sanity-restoring item for Wortox and Warly, as the sanity gained over time is not decreased by their food penalties.
  • This recipe is an effective method of restoring sanity in Winter. Ice is easily available from Mini Glaciers. Forget-Me-Lots will grow on empty farm plots, even in Winter. Honey can be stocked up in advance thanks to its long shelf life, or obtained every day by hammering the Gigantic Beehive.
  • As the sanity-over-time effect is not affected by spoilage, a spoilt Soothing Tea will have a net gain of 20 sanity.
  • It can be more effective to farm Toma Root and Onion for Salsa Fresca, instead of farming Forget-Me-Lots.

Trivia

  • The Soothing Tea was introduced in the Reap What You Sow update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"sweettea"
“A nice cup of tea to forget all my problems.”
–Wilson
“What's so great about it? It's just leaf juice.”
–Willow
“Ahhh... is nice.”
–Wolfgang
“Would you care for a tea party, Abigail?”
–Wendy
“THEY'RE TRYING TO MAKE ME LET DOWN MY GUARD... IT WILL NOT WORK!”
–WX-78
“It's as if all my worries and cares are fading away.”
–Wickerbottom
“Almost makes me forget where I am.”
–Woodie
“I can't even remember the last time I had a decent cup of tea.”
–Maxwell
“A warriör döes nöt partake in "tea time".”
–Wigfrid
“Mother used to make tea for me... at least I think she did...”
–Webber
“Ah... je me sens relaxé.”
–Warly
“Mmmm... ahhh”
–Wormwood
“I've got no time for tea! There's work to be done, like... er...”
–Winona
“A little sip will do the trick.”
–Wortox
“Mmm... is nice.”
–Wurt
“It makes my head feel a bit fuzzy... but I'm sure that's fine!”
–Walter
“Well... I guess there's time for a quick cup of tea.”
–Wanda

The '''Soothing Tea''' is a recipe exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in the ''[[Return of Them]]'' update. It is made with 1 [[Forget-Me-Lots]], 1 [[Honey]] or [[Honeycomb]], 1 [[Ice]], and 1 Filler that cannot be [[Meats]], [[Fish]], [[Monster Food]], [[Egg]], [[Dairy]], [[Inedible]] or [[Vegetable]]. A convenient filler would be more of the required ingredients or [[Fruit]]. The Forget-Me-Lots can be fresh or [[Dried Forget-Me-Lots]].

It restores 3 [[health]] and 15 [[sanity]] and an additional 30 sanity over 1 minute. It increases the temperature perceived by the player by 1 degree per second over 5 seconds.
__NOTOC__
== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Forget-Me-Lots All
|item2 = Sweetener
|item3 = Ices
|item4 = Filler
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Soothing Tea
|note = Filler cannot be [[Meats]], [[Fish]], [[Monster Food]], [[Egg]], [[Dairy]], [[Inedible]] or [[Vegetable]].
}}

|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Soothing Tea
}}

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Except for [[Ice Cream]], this dish has the highest efficiency in terms of sanity gain per honey. 
* Soothing Tea is a useful sanity-restoring item for [[Wortox]] and [[Warly]], as the sanity gained over time is not decreased by their food penalties.
*This recipe is an effective method of restoring sanity in [[Seasons/Winter|Winter]]. Ice is easily available from [[Mini Glacier|Mini Glaciers]]. Forget-Me-Lots will grow on empty farm plots, even in Winter. Honey can be stocked up in advance thanks to its long shelf life, or obtained every day by hammering the [[Gigantic Beehive]].
*As the sanity-over-time effect is not affected by spoilage, a spoilt Soothing Tea will have a net gain of 20 sanity.
*It can be more effective to farm [[Toma Root]] and [[Onion]] for [[Salsa Fresca]], instead of farming Forget-Me-Lots.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}}Trivia ==
* The Soothing Tea was introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Reap What You Sow|Reap What You Sow]]'' update.
